Title: Genetic modification of flavone biosynthesis in rice enhances biofilm formation of soil diazotrophic bacteria and biological nitrogen fixation

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stimulates biofilm formation of diazotrophic bacteria, enhances bacterial colonization and biological nitrogen fixation, and supports grain yield under low nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CYP75B3 together with CYP75B4 increased apigenin and apigenin O-glucoside in roots/root exudates, enhanced bacterial biofilm induction and nitrogen fixation, and increased seed yield under nitrogen limitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in leaves by qRT-PCR; CRISPR lines showed little transcript
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf transcripts strongly reduced in CRISPR knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme; key control for diverting flux away from apigenin toward luteolin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; sequencing of edited alleles; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling of roots and exudates; bacterial biofilm assays; in vivo 15N2 incorporation; yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP75B3 was simultaneously knocked out with CYP75B4 in rice using CRISPR/Cas9. 2. The edited lines accumulated more apigenin and apigenin O-glucoside in roots and root exudates. 3. These changes promoted diazotroph biofilm formation, increased in vivo biological nitrogen fixation, and improved grain yield under limiting nitrogen.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low nitrogen conditions (22.5 ppm N; 30% nitrogen medium); hydroponic and soil growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon profiling of root-associated microbiota; raw FASTQ available at figshare dataset/raw_FASTQ/17147837
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TaU3 promoter for multiplex gRNA cassette; 35S terminator in CRISPR construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout/editing of CYP75B3 together with CYP75B4 is a promising strategy to increase root apigenin exudation, recruit diazotrophs, improve biological nitrogen fixation and reduce fertilizer demand in cereals
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height; altered flavone profile including decreased other flavones such as tricin; possible impacts on resistance to bacterial or fungal infection require further study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study experimentally assessed the double knockout, not CYP75B3 alone; effect is mediated through altered root exudate chemistry rather than direct nitrogen metabolism
